## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Youngstown,_Ohio&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Transportation")]

### Road

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Youngstown,_Ohio&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Road")]

The city is directly served by [Interstate 680](/wiki/Interstate_680_(Ohio) "Interstate 680 (Ohio)"), which connects the city with [Interstate 80](/wiki/Interstate_80_in_Ohio "Interstate 80 in Ohio"), [Interstate 76](/wiki/Interstate_76_in_Ohio "Interstate 76 in Ohio") (the [Ohio Turnpike](/wiki/Ohio_Turnpike "Ohio Turnpike")), and [Ohio State Route 11](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_11 "Ohio State Route 11"). I-680 begins at a junction with I-80 and SR 11, the start of the latter two routes' concurrency, in [Austintown Township](/wiki/Austintown_Township,_Ohio "Austintown Township, Ohio"). Traveling southeast, I-680 passes through Youngstown, exiting southward from the city.

At I-680 exit 4A in the city, a freeway called the "Madison Avenue Expressway" begins.[[199]](#cite_note-WKBN20140508-199) It first carries [SR 193](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_193 "Ohio State Route 193"); at its second exit, the state route leaves the freeway and [U.S. Route 422](/wiki/U.S._Route_422_in_Ohio "U.S. Route 422 in Ohio") joins. The Madison Avenue Expressway continues until reaching a junction with [U.S. 62](/wiki/U.S._Route_62_in_Ohio "U.S. Route 62 in Ohio") and [SR 7](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_7 "Ohio State Route 7"); after the junction, U.S. 422 comes to an at-grade intersection with Oak Street. At I-680 southbound exit 6B (northbound exit 6) in the city, a freeway called the "Himrod Avenue Expressway" begins; it carries US 62 and SR 7.[[199]](#cite_note-WKBN20140508-199) The Himrod Avenue Expressway continues for over 1 mile (1.6 km) until reaching the same junction with the Madison Avenue Expressway (US 422); after the junction, U.S. 62 and SR 7 come to an at-grade intersection with Albert Street. Taken together, the Madison Avenue Expressway, the Himrod Avenue Expressway and the section of I-680 between the two form a freeway loop around [Downtown Youngstown](/wiki/Downtown_Youngstown "Downtown Youngstown") and the [Youngstown State University](/wiki/Youngstown_State_University "Youngstown State University") campus.

The [SR 711](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_711 "Ohio State Route 711") freeway begins at a junction with I-680 in Youngstown and runs northeast and north out of the city and Mahoning County before terminating at a junction with I-80 and SR 11, the end of the latter two routes' concurrency, in [Liberty Township](/wiki/Liberty_Township,_Trumbull_County,_Ohio "Liberty Township, Trumbull County, Ohio"). In addition to non-freeway sections of US 62, US 422, SR 7 and SR 193, state routes [SR 170](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_170 "Ohio State Route 170"), [SR 289](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_289 "Ohio State Route 289"), [SR 616](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_616 "Ohio State Route 616") and [SR 625](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_625 "Ohio State Route 625") serve the city. Ohio State Route 11, a north–south freeway, runs to the west of Youngstown. The Ohio Turnpike passes to the west and south of Youngstown.

In January 2025, the Eastgate Regional Council of Governments [metropolitan planning organization](/wiki/Metropolitan_planning_organization "Metropolitan planning organization") received a $979,000 federal grant to study the possible conversion of a 1.5-mile (2.4 km) stretch of the Madison Avenue Expressway between Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard ([SR 289](/wiki/Ohio_State_Route_289 "Ohio State Route 289")) and Andrews Avenue from a trenched freeway to a low-speed surface boulevard suitable for motorized vehicles, bicycles and pedestrians. The council says that the roadway infrastructure is deteriorating, the neighborhood split by the freeway would be reconnected and that 25 to 30 acres (10 to 12 ha) of land would be made available for new purposes. The plan could take 10 to 15 years to implement. Critics suggest that the council is not operating in the best interests of the city and that the transportation options provided by the freeway are beneficial to residents.[[200]](#cite_note-200)[[201]](#cite_note-201)[[202]](#cite_note-202)[[203]](#cite_note-203)

### Public transit

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Youngstown,_Ohio&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Public transit")]

The Youngstown area is served by the [Western Reserve Transit Authority](/wiki/Western_Reserve_Transit_Authority "Western Reserve Transit Authority") (WRTA) bus system, which is supported through Mahoning County property and sales taxes. WRTA, whose main terminal is in the downtown area, provides service throughout the city and into surrounding Mahoning and Trumbull counties. The downtown terminal serves as the Youngstown area's [Greyhound](/wiki/Greyhound_Lines "Greyhound Lines") terminal.[[204]](#cite_note-204)

### Air

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Youngstown,_Ohio&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Air")]

The [Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport](/wiki/Youngstown-Warren_Regional_Airport "Youngstown-Warren Regional Airport") is the primary airport for the region. As of 2022, no commercial airlines serve the airport, with [Allegiant Air](/wiki/Allegiant_Air "Allegiant Air") ending service on January 4, 2018.[[205]](#cite_note-205) The airport is home to the [Youngstown Air Reserve Station](/wiki/Youngstown_Air_Reserve_Station "Youngstown Air Reserve Station") and [910th Airlift Wing](/wiki/910th_Airlift_Wing "910th Airlift Wing").[[206]](#cite_note-206) Smaller general aviation airports in the city and vicinity include [Lansdowne Airport](/wiki/Lansdowne_Airport "Lansdowne Airport") and [Youngstown Elser Metro Airport](/wiki/Youngstown_Elser_Metro_Airport "Youngstown Elser Metro Airport").

### Rail

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Youngstown,_Ohio&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Rail")]

In the vicinity of the WRTA terminal is a former [Baltimore and Ohio Railroad](/wiki/Baltimore_and_Ohio_Railroad "Baltimore and Ohio Railroad") station. The [historic terminal building](/wiki/Baltimore_%26_Ohio_Station_(Youngstown) "Baltimore & Ohio Station (Youngstown)") served B&O trains until 1971. Since converted into a banquet hall, it was a station along [Amtrak](/wiki/Amtrak "Amtrak")'s *[Three Rivers](/wiki/Three_Rivers_(train) "Three Rivers (train)")* between Chicago and New York from 1995 to 2005.[[207]](#cite_note-207) The nearest Amtrak service is the *[Capitol Limited](/wiki/Capitol_Limited "Capitol Limited")* at [Alliance station](/wiki/Alliance_station "Alliance station") 42 miles to the southwest. Freight rail lines owned by [CSX Transportation](/wiki/CSX_Transportation "CSX Transportation"), [Norfolk Southern Railway](/wiki/Norfolk_Southern_Railway "Norfolk Southern Railway"), and the [Youngstown and Southeastern Railroad](/wiki/Youngstown_and_Southeastern_Railroad "Youngstown and Southeastern Railroad") still go through the area.

### Rideshare

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Youngstown,_Ohio&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Rideshare")]

On June 23, 2016, [Uber](/wiki/Uber "Uber") launched services in Youngstown, covering all of Mahoning County and most of Trumbull County.[[208]](#cite_note-208)
